segments.text: Keep justice, and do righteousness, For My salvation is about to come, And My righteousness to be revealed.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Blessed is the man who does this, And the son of man who lays hold on it; Who keeps from defiling the Sabbath, And keeps his hand from doing any evil.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The Lord has utterly separated me from His people
segments.speaker: Son of the foreigner

segments.text: Here I am, a dry tree.
segments.speaker: Eunuch

segments.text: To the eunuchs who keep My Sabbaths, And choose what pleases Me, And hold fast My covenant,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Even to them I will give in My house And within My walls a place and a name Better than that of sons and daughters; I will give them an everlasting name That shall not be cut off.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Also the sons of the foreigner Who join themselves to the Lord, to serve Him, And to love the name of the Lord, to be His servants— Everyone who keeps from defiling the Sabbath, And holds fast My covenant—
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Even them I will bring to My holy mountain, And make them joyful in My house of prayer. Their burnt offerings and their sacrifices Will be accepted on My altar; For My house shall be called a house of prayer for all nations.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The Lord God, who gathers the outcasts of Israel, says:
Yet I will gather to him Others besides those who are gathered to him.
segments.speaker: The Lord God

segments.text: All you beasts of the field, come to devour, All you beasts in the forest.
His watchmen are blind, They are all ignorant; They are all dumb dogs, They cannot bark; Sleeping, lying down, loving to slumber.
Yes, they are greedy dogs Which never have enough. And they are shepherds Who cannot understand; They all look to their own way, Every one for his own gain, From his own territory.
Come, I will bring wine, And we will fill ourselves with intoxicating drink; Tomorrow will be as today, And much more abundant.
segments.speaker: Unknown
